config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. Society matters a lot, but I do not think its interests should automatically come before the rights of the individual. In my view, rights are the boundary that protects people from abuse by the majority, the state, or powerful groups. Society is strongest when it serves people without erasing their dignity and freedom.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus selalu didahulukan di atas hak individu. Dari perspektif saya sebagai orang Amerika, hak individu justru menjadi pagar penting agar mayoritas atau pemerintah tidak mudah menginjak kebebasan orang yang lebih lemah. Kepentingan publik memang penting, tetapi kalau hak dasar bisa dikorbankan setiap kali dianggap "demi masyarakat", itu membuka jalan bagi penyalahgunaan kekuasaan. Menurut saya, masyarakat yang sehat adalah masyarakat yang melindungi individu sambil tetap mencari kebaikan bersama, bukan yang menuntut individu selalu mengalah.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I disagree with the statement because in Indonesia I have seen how easily “the public interest” can be used to silence weaker people or excuse unfair treatment. Society does matter, but it is not automatically wiser than the individual, and majority opinion can be wrong, oppressive, or corrupt. The right balance is not to sacrifice individual rights first, but to protect them while still allowing limited public action when there is a clear and justified common need. If rights are treated as secondary by default, then the people with the least power are usually the ones who pay the price.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu karena hak individu tidak boleh dianggap nomor dua hanya karena sesuatu disebut “kepentingan masyarakat.” Dari pengalaman nilai-nilai Amerika, saya melihat hak individu sebagai batas penting terhadap tirani mayoritas, birokrasi, dan kekuasaan yang gampang melampaui wewenang. Tentu masyarakat butuh ketertiban dan tujuan bersama, tetapi kalau hak dasar bisa dikorbankan setiap kali ada alasan kolektif, maka yang paling rentan justru paling mudah dirugikan. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang baik bukan yang selalu menuntut individu mengalah, melainkan yang mampu mengejar kebaikan bersama tanpa menghapus kebebasan dasar orang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I still disagree with the statement: in my view, society’s interests should not automatically take priority over individual rights. From an Indonesian perspective, I have seen how “for the common good” can become a convenient excuse for pressure from officials, crowds, or powerful groups, while ordinary people have little protection. Public order and shared goals matter, but they should be pursued within clear limits, because once rights become negotiable by default, the weakest people are the first to suffer. A healthy society is one that can protect the common good without treating the individual as disposable.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu karena bagi saya, kepentingan masyarakat tidak boleh otomatis mengalahkan hak individu. Sebagai orang Amerika, saya melihat hak individu sebagai pagar utama terhadap tirani mayoritas, birokrasi yang berlebihan, dan kekuasaan yang suka melampaui batas atas nama “kebaikan bersama.” Argumen bahwa masyarakat butuh ketertiban memang benar, tetapi kalau hak dasar bisa dikorbankan setiap kali ada alasan kolektif, yang paling rentan justru akan paling mudah dirugikan. Menurut saya, masyarakat yang sehat adalah masyarakat yang mengejar kepentingan umum tanpa menjadikan individu sebagai korban yang bisa dikorbankan kapan saja.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
